Figure 36. Plan Category for External Costs Upload

The important parameter is that you allow the import of plan data. In addition, you must define a separate virtual plan category to be used later in EBRR customizing.

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> General Controlling -> Planning -> Assign Plan Categories to a Virtual Category

IMG Object

FCOMV_CAT_ASSGMT

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11002399

Table/View/ViewCluster

FCOMC_CAT_ASSGMT

Figure 37. Virtual Plan Category (Costs - External Upload, Revenues - Billing Plan)

 

5.3 Customizing – Event-Based Revenue Recognition

EBRR (Event-Based Revenue Recognition) postings are triggered by CO (COIN) of FI (RWIN) source documents only. Please find examples of such documents below:

  • COIN – CO Document
    • Activity Allocation (KB21n, IW41)
  • RWIN – FI Document
    • FI document (like FB01, FB60)
    • SD Billing (VF01)
    • Material Consumption (MIGO, 261 Movement Type)

 

5.3.1 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Sources

The first check/filter is the relevance of a document item GL account for EBRR post-processing.

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Sources

IMG Object

FINS_TRR

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11000209

Table/View/ViewCluster

FINS_TRR_SOURCE

In our example, I simplify the setup because it is not specific to our particular use case. If you are looking for more advanced knowledge, please refer to the following resources:

  • SAP Note 2778479
  • SAP Note 3259335
  • Configuration of G/L Accounts in Event-Based Revenue Recognition

Figure 38. EBRR Source Configuration for Costs Selection

Figure 39. EBRR Source Configuration for Revenues Selections

 

5.3.2 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Assignment Rules

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Assignment Rules

IMG Object

FINS_TRR

Transaction Code

SM34 (FINS_TRR)

Table/View/ViewCluster

FINS_TRR_ASS

You can reuse already existing rules or create a new one.

Figure 40. EBRR GL Posting Rules Configuration

You can see the relevant GL accounts with descriptions below:

Table 3. List of Relevant EBRR GL Accounts

 

5.3.3 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Recognition Keys

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Recognition Keys

IMG Object

FINS_TRR

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11000209

Table/View/View Cluster

FINS_TRR_VAL

Figure 41. EBRR Revenue Recognition Key Configuration - General Data

  • The key is activated for the necessary company codes and accounting principles.
  • The field Revenue Recognition method is “3 – Cost Based POC”.
  • Please use your customized assignment rule and plan category.
  • The source for plan values should be “500 :Cost and revenue planning of all objects from ACDOCP ”
  • The field “Billable Determination method” is always empty (not relevant for services).

Figure 42. EBRR Revenue Recognition Key Configuration - Company Code & Accounting Principle Specific Data

 

5.3.4 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Document Types

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Document Types

IMG Object

FINS_TRR

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11000209

Table/View/ViewCluster

FINS_TRR_ACP

Figure 43. EBRR - Document Types Configuration

Fields Description:

  • Plan Category - Plan Category for history of plan used by EBRR.
    • Description: The values for this Plan Category display the history of plan values used by the closing or re-evaluation of Event-Based Revenue Recognition.
    • Use: The history is recorded with respect to the financial period and the cost elements for the adjustment postings of recognized COGS and revenue.
    • Dependencies: A Plan category of the application type 03, Revenue Recognition should be used here. 
  • IFRS 15-Indicator (Not Relevant for Current Use Case) – No impact on our use case, not supported for Service Documents. 
  • Currency Handling (Not Relevant for Current Use Case)  - The various possibilities of currency handling offer to choose the
    • principal currency, which is the main currency of the closing run of revenue recognition.
    • currency calculations, which allow to evaluate the total balance sheet amounts and to separate currency effects.
    • base currency of currency calculations, which is the starting point of the currency translations for the currency calculations.
    • document currency of closing, which is the document currency used for the financial documents created by the closing run of event-based revenue recognition.
    • valuation date, which is the suggested valuation date for all financial documents of event-based revenue recognition.
  • Loss handling (Not Relevant for Current Use Case) - This function determines the loss handling for POC-type recognition methods. If a plan loss is expected, the planned loss should be posted as a loss provision into the income statement immediately.

 

5.3.5 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Evaluation Scope

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Evaluation Scope incl. Financial Statement

IMG Object

FINS_TRR

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11000209

Table/View/ViewCluster

FINS_TRR_EVA

For our use case, the scope is 2: Realtime evaluation and re-evaluation at closing.

Figure 44. EBRR - Evaluation Scope incl. Financial Statement Configuration

5.3.6 Derivation of Recognition Key for Service Documents

The revenue recognition key derivation is based on the table below. We need to add our new service contract "root" item category to the list. 

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Derivation of Recognition Key for Service Documents

IMG Object

FINS_TRR_DRK_CRM

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11001107

Table/View/ViewCluster

FINS_TRR_DRK_CRM

Figure 45. Revenue Recognition Derivation Configuration

entry.title: Customer Engagement Initiative 2025 on Business Event Logging
entry.content:
As part of the new CEI cycle for 2025 we launched a CEI project for Business Event Logging. Registration is open until March 28, 2025.

Business Event Logging allows you to create event logs in SAP S/4HANA systems and on SAP BTP ABAP Environment, based on Business Events and on Change Documents. The logs can be used to evaluate changes to your business documents, and to analyze your business processes with the help of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

Register here until March 28, 2025: Insights into Business Processes with Business Event Logging 

We plan to roll-out information about the usage of Business Event Logging, and collect your feedback and ideas.

Find more information about Business Event Logging here:

  • One Tool to Track Them All 
  • Using Customer-Defined Events in Business Event Logging
  • Integrating Business Event Logging with SAP Signavio
  • Transform your Business Processes with SAP Signavio and SAP Build 

Looking forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: Key User Custom Fields in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise: Dos and Don’ts
entry.content:
Teaser

Did you ever wonder how transporting key user custom fields works in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise and which apps you should use to avoid common mistakes? 
Do you want to know what to consider regarding key user custom fields when you upgrade your S/4HANA system? 
If your answer is yes, then this blog post is just for you. 

 

Disclaimer

Please note that this blog does exclusively address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise and does not address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
In this blog we use S/4HANA as an abbreviation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise. In addition, for key user custom fields we use the abbreviation custom fields.

 

Table of Contents

This blog post covers the following topics: 

  • Dos and Don’ts in the context of software logistics
  • What to consider regarding custom fields in upgrade situations 
  • General Dos and Don’ts regarding custom fields 
  • Dos and Don’ts regarding ABAP-managed fields (abbreviation AMF) 

 

Dos and Don’ts in the context of software logistics

When working with key user custom fields in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you use "software collections" to transport your custom fields. These collections act like a safety box, wrapping the technical transport process. This is different in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise. There, you transport the custom field artifacts on your own using a standard transport mechanism. With this, you have more freedom, but there are many things that could go wrong. It can easily happen that the managed and unmanaged objects of a custom field aren't kept in sync, by forgetting to transport the unmanaged objects, for example. 

Here is a list of Dos SAP recommends: 

  • Use Fiori apps for transport handling (also refer to note 2660797)
    • Register Extensions for Transport to
      • assign key user items to a package 
      • assign custom fields to a transport 
    • Configure Software Packages to  
      • configure automatic transport or task handling for packages 
      • assign transports to packages 
  • Register a so-called job user for generation processes (also refer to SAP Help Portal). You must ensure that: 
    • the job user is provided in maintenance view ATO_PK_JOB_USER (transaction SM30) 
    • the job user has sufficient authorization for publishing (e.g. SAP_ALL profile) 
    • the job user is valid (exists in the correct client and is within validity range) 
    • operations (save/publish/delete) are performed in the correct client 

And here is a list of Don’ts: 

  • Never develop custom fields in several different development systems – use one development system only 
    • Implemented checks are system-specific. This means that no data of other systems is considered 
    • Generated artifact names depend on custom field names, which means that a clash of object names may occur 
  • Never edit custom fields in several systems. Only edit them in one development system: 
    • Custom fields and its generated artifacts have a dedicated source system. This means that there is a risk of system inconsistency in case of custom field deletion if generated objects exist only locally 

What to consider regarding custom fields in upgrade situations

There is one more crucial difference regarding lifecycle stability for custom fields when working in S/4HANA compared to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the upgrade procedure contains a task where custom field artifacts are technically adapted on the fly, due to changes in the SAP standard.  
This is different from the upgrade procedure in S/4HANA. In S/4HANA, there is a custom field analysis process figuring out whether an adaptation is necessary. If this process identifies an adaptation need, you'll see the following messages in the upgrade log: 

Message message id 'CFD_REGENERATE_OP' message number '001' ('Regeneration of Custom fields necessary; refer to SAP Note 2640715') 

Please note that the following message could be shown, too: 

Message message id 'CFD_REGENERATE_OP' message number '016' ('Regeneration of datasource extension necessary; refer to SAP Note 2640715') 

This second message tells you that the issue is not an issue with a custom field but rather with a key user datasource extension object. You can find more details in note 2640715.  

Please note that there's a difference between upgrading the development system or a test or production system. 

In case you are upgrading the development system and an adaptation need has been identified, you should run the following report: CFD_REGENERATE_IN_ONPREMISE. This report implements the needed adaptations. When you use it, make sure to always be on the newest note level of this report. 

In case you are upgrading a system different from the development system, and an adaptation need has been identified, don't run the report. Instead, import the adaptations which have been made in the development system. 

In some scenarios, things can get a bit more complicated. For instance, you might want to run a test upgrade. To do this, you copy the development system, giving it a different system ID. Alternatively, you might need to move your development system to another system, resulting in a new system ID. In these cases, it's important to note that custom fields are only editable in their original source system. They are not editable in other systems. To enable their editability, you have to follow note 3484189.  
Only do this in the exceptional situations described above. Never maintain your custom fields in more than one system. If you maintain your custom fields in more than one system, they will break. 

 

General Dos and Don’ts regarding custom fields 

There may be situations where you created a custom field with a certain name (custom field ID) and accidentally deleted it. Now, you want to re-create the custom field with exactly the same name of the deleted one. 
This can be done. First, check the context you're in: 

  • If the transport with the field creation has not been released, you can delete it and recreate the field, even when choosing an incompatible field type. 
  • If the transport with the field creation has been released, you can delete it and recreate the field only as long as the field type you chose is compatible (see also note 3328541) 

It may happen that you want to expose your custom field in an OData service (to make the field visible in a UI that gets its data from this OData service, for example) and this OData service can't be enabled in the Custom Fields app. In this case, the respective OData service is not enabled for custom fields. To solve this, you can create a respective extensibility enabling request via the customer influence channel. 
Please note that using an OData redefinition approach to add custom fields to OData service entities doesn't work and is not supported (Note 1574568 constraint 34). 

 

Dos and Don’ts in the context of ABAP-managed fields 

These Dos and Don'ts are relevant for you if you use the feature ABAP-Managed Fields (described based on a concrete example in the blog post Convert Manually Created Append Fields to Real Key User Custom Fields). ABAP-managed fields allow you to convert your manually created append fields into real key user custom fields.
The challenges regarding software logistics come from the fact that for ABAP-managed fields, some of the custom fields' artefacts are generated and managed by the Custom Fields framework, and some are customer-managed (such as the CDS Code View, the CDS Text View, data elements, append, and so on). As these customer-managed artifacts are not safe-guarded and controlled by the Custom Fields framework, one could easily break the custom field by changing these manually created objects in an incompatible way, or accidentally deleting them.  

Are ABAP-managed fields lifecycle-stable, and do they follow the clean core principles?

The answer is yes. Let's have a closer look at the details.  
An ABAP-managed field comes with a set of unmanaged objects: 

  • Data element and domain (manually created or SAP standard data element) 
  • Manually created append with the field type using the data element above 
  • Depending on the chosen semantic type, also a manually created CDS code view and CDS text view  

If all of these unmanaged objects are built with ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development, they automatically apply to the clean core rules. The ABAP-managed field is 100% clean core compliant and fully lifecycle-stable. 

In general, within your unmanaged objects, it's possible that you use non-C1 released objects. With that, you can't fully follow the clean core path any longer. 

Let's dive a little deeper into the unmanaged objects to better understand how critical certain things are in terms of upgrade stability: 

Data element and domain

If the data element or domain you use to type your ABAP-managed field is not an SAP standard object which is released for Use in Cloud Development, then this data element or domain could be changed incompatibly or even deleted with any updated version of S/4HANA. However, in a lot of cases it’s unlikely that this will happen because, for example, the data element is prominently used within the SAP standard (which means that it's used for the typing of key fields.) 

Append field

In some cases, the Extension Include Structure to which you add the field using an ABAP dictionary append is released for Extensibility in Cloud Development. Then, you can add this append in ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development. In case the Extension Include Structure is not released for developers but only for key users, you can't add the DDIC append in ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development but only in Standard ABAP. This no longer completely follows the clean core principle, but can still be 100% upgrade-stable, as it’s protected with the C0-contract release for key users. Using ABAP language version Standard, there are no more lifecycle stability checks in place to prevent you from leaving the upgrade-stable way. Consider the following aspects to be 100% upgrade-stable: 

  • Make sure that you use ZZ as prefix of the field name, or your own customer namespace to avoid collisions with future standard fields or your own custom fields 
  • Make sure that you’re using the field suffix as defined in the respective business context in transaction SCFD_REGISTRY

CDS Code View and CDS Text View

In case your CDS view references SAP standard objects which are not released for Use in Cloud Development (CDS views, data elements, database tables, …) these objects may be changed incompatibly or even deleted with an updated version of S/4HANA. In this case, 100% upgrade stability is no longer guaranteed. 

In the worst case, if one of the non-C1-released SAP standard objects you reference has been changed incompatibly or has been deleted, it can cause issues during a system upgrade. You’d have to rework your affected unmanaged objects to solve these issues. 

You can get more information regarding ABAP-managed fields following these links:  

  • General handling: SAP note 2981484 
  • Enable ABAP-managed fields for database tables with proxy views: SAP note 2463257 
  • Re-creating a custom field: SAP note 3327309 

Golden rules when working with ABAP-managed fields

  • Use the ATO apps for transport handling (also refer to note 2660797) 
  • Only use ABAP-managed fields in the exceptional situations described above 
  • Use a suitable prefix for the append field (ZZ or a real namespace) 
  • Use a suitable suffix for the append field depending on the business context 
  • Ensure that these objects are kept in the same software component as the append structure with the database field itself 
  • Ensure that the append field also exists in subsequent systems. Either the append field is imported before or transported together with the custom field 
  • Never delete append fields via SE11 before disabling the custom field via SCFD_EUI 
  • Never modify or delete the value help view used in the AMF field of type List 
  • Never delete the data element used in the AMF field 
  • Never continue with the publishing or deletion of further custom fields if a previous publishing or deletion failed 

Now, you are done! Congratulations! With this, you are now equipped to 

  • handle software logistics for custom fields 
  • avoid common pitfalls in this context. 

Please note that this blog has been elaborated by a team of experts, and I want to 
thank Yasmina van Rooij (YasminavanRooij), Daniel Wachs (DanielWachs), and Georg Wilhelm (georg_wilhelm) for their extremely helpful contributions.

 

Feedback, Comments, or Questions

You’re cordially invited to provide feedback, comments, or questions in the comments section below. Please follow our profiles to get updates when we publish more posts on these topics. 
Feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/). 

Other related blog posts: 

  1. Convert Manually Created Append Fields to Real Key User Custom Fields 
  2. How to implement a custom field of type “Code List based on CDS view” with a developer extensibility custom entity 
    How to implement a custom entity in the ABAP RESTful Programming Model using remote function modules    
  3. How to configure the value help as a context-dependent value help 
  4. How to configure intent-based navigation on a key user custom field of type “Code List based on CDS View”    
    and in this case, how to easily find the fitting semantic object and semantic object parameter: Custom field type “Code list based on CDS view”: Finding/defining the right value help view and the right Semantic Object and Semantic Object Parameter for Intent-Based Navigation 
  5. Custom Fields in S/4HANA: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility 
  6. Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties (mandatory,...) 
  7. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties 
  8. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding 
  9. Custom fields in S/4HANA: Key User versus Developer Extensibility 
  10. SAP Screen Personas: How to position key user custom fields exactly where you want them to be

entry.title: How to: Extension of WebDynpro Configurations due to changes in CDS Views
entry.content:
The backend and WebDynPro must be properly configured to enable a field for filtering in a WebDynpro application. This involves modifying the CDS (Core Data Services) views to ensure the field is recognised and available for filtering in the frontend configuration.

This topic concerns these Fiori Apps, but the principle is the same for each WebDynpro App.

CDS Query (object)CDS Query (2C+SQL View)CDS Query WebDynpro ConfigurationApp ID
C_CostCenterQ2001CFICOSTCTRQ2001FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTCCF0963
C_CostCenterPlanActQ2001CFICCPLANACT2001FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTCC01W0081
C_ProfitCenterQ2701CFIPROFCTRQ2701FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTPCF0959
C_ProfitCenterPlanActQ2701CFIPCPLANACT2701FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTPC01W0079
C_MarketSegmentQ2501CFIMARKSEGMQ2501FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTMSF0960
C_MarketSegmentPlanActQ2501CFIMSPLANACT2501FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTMS01W0078
C_ProfitAndLossQ2901CFIPROFLOSSQ2901FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTPLF0958
C_ProfitAndLossPlanActQ2903CFIPLPLANACTFIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTPL03F1710
C_FunctionalAreasQ2801CFIFAQ2801FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTFAF1583A
C_FunctionalAreaPlanActQ2801CFIFAPLANACT2801FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTFA01W0077
C_InternalOrderPlanActQ2101CFIIOPLANACT2101FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTIO01W0076
C_SalesOrderQ2301CFISALESORDQ2301FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTSOF1582A
C_ProjectQ2201CFIPROJECTQ2201FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTPR F0961
C_ProjectPlanActQ2201CFIPRPLANACT2201FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTPR01W0080

Backend changes in CDS views:

Variant 1: Extending the CDS View

If the field is not yet available for filtering, you must modify the CDS view and add the appropriate annotations.

  •  Create a new CDS view extension and ensure you use your naming conventions where required.

 Important: Replace placeholders such as ZXXCOSTEXT,  C_CostCenterQ2001, ZXX_C_COST_CENTER_EXT, and I_JournalEntryItemCube.SourceLedger with names relevant to your implementation. These correspond to:

  • Catalog name
  • CDS view name
  • CDS extension view name
  • Field name

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXCOSTEXT'
extend view C_CostCenterQ2001 with ZXX_C_COST_CENTER_EXT
{
@Consumption.filter: { selectionType: #SINGLE, multipleSelections: true, mandatory: false }
I_JournalEntryItemCube.SourceLedger

}

 

  • Save and activate the CDS view

This will add Source Ledger to the C_CostCenterQ2001 query as a Filter. However, to see it within the Fiori App, you need to change the configuration.

Variant 2: Using Metadata Extension

If the field already exists in a standard CDS view but does not have a filter annotation, you can use a metadata extension instead of modifying the view directly:

  • Ensure Metadata Extensions Are Allowed (If not, you will need to use Variant 1.).
  • If you do not have this annotation present in the queries mentioned above, you will need to install this SAP Note 3509451 to your system.
  • Create a Metadata Extension

Important: Replace placeholders such as CostCenterQ2001 and CalendarWeek with names relevant to your implementation. These correspond to:

  • CDS view name
  • Field name

 

 

@Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER
annotate entity C_CostCenterQ2001
    with 
{
    @Consumption.filter: { selectionType: #SINGLE, multipleSelections: true, mandatory: false }
    CalendarWeek;
    
}

 

I would suggest you use @Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER, so it is determined what was done.

WebDynpro configuration:

  • Open the Fiori application Cost Centers – Actuals. Right-click in the filter area and select ‘Technical Help’.  Technical Help window will open.

  • In the technical help window, click on the shown area (Application Configuration).  A new window will open.

  • In the application configuration window, click on the Configuration Name.  You will be redirected to the Page Schema.

  • Scroll down to locate the Overview Page Schema. Expand the Page Header Area and then click on Config ID.

  • In the newly opened window, drag the desired filter into the Page Header Area within the schema below.

  • Click save.

Once all these steps are done you will see the field in the search filter among other fields like Ledger, Fiscal Year of Ledger etc., the position on where the field appears is up to you.

 

I would like to say a big thanks to my colleague, who helped me with creating this blog and preparing the documentation.

entry.title: The Case for Upgrades in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - New whitepaper published
entry.content:
Is your business still running an older version of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition? You could be missing out on critical innovations and putting your system at risk. Friendly reminder: Mainstream and extended maintenance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems on versions 1709, 1809, 1909, and 2020 will conclude in December 2025. Now is the perfect time to plan your upgrade and stay ahead with the latest features and support!

 

Read the whitepaper, "Embrace agility and upgrade for success."

 

 Why Upgrade? It’s More Than Just a Task

Upgrading your SAP system isn't just about keeping up; it's about gaining a strategic advantage. Think of it as future-proofing your business. Here's what you stand to gain:

  • Supercharged Security: Outdated systems are vulnerable. Regular upgrades keep you protected with the latest cybersecurity measures and data protection. SAP only guarantees security updates for the most recent feature and support packages, so staying current is essential.
  • Innovation at Your Fingertips: Running on the latest release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition gives you access to continuous innovation, including advanced business AI, automation, and predictive analytics. New features like the AI copilot Joule, Microsoft Teams integration, and SAP Fiori improvements boost productivity and user experience. (see full list here). These innovations are included in your subscription—make sure you don’t miss out!
  • Cut Costs and Complexity: Sticking with older versions often leads to costly and complex custom code. Regular upgrades in combination with a clean core approach can help you stay closer to the SAP standard, making your system more agile and easier to manage.
  • Maximize Your ROI: You've already invested in SAP. By upgrading, you're maximizing that investment. You're unlocking the full potential of your system and getting the most value out of your license.
  • Practice makes Perfect: It's like training a muscle. The more often you upgrade, the easier the process becomes. Regular upgrades equip your team with the know-how for faster, smoother projects with fewer disruptions. Customers like Hitachi High-Tech have reduced their upgrade projects to just a single month by adopting a regular upgrade approach. Read Hitachi´s complete upgrade story.

Build your Upgrade Routine:

Establishing your governance strategy and plan to fit your individual operation and needs is key. SAP recommends that you run on the latest release or at least one below the latest, aligning your IT change management and business release strategy, as well as your project schedule, with SAP S/4HANA release timelines. As announced with the new SAP S/4HANA release and maintenance strategy, new releases are delivered every second year, with three feature and support packages in between, offering you greater flexibility and easier-to-consume innovation.

SAP is Here to Help You Every Step of the Way

Upgrading doesn't have to be daunting. SAP provides a wealth of resources to ensure a smooth transition:

  • SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Upgrade, a methodology for implementing SAP solutions, guides your team through the upgrade by prompting deliverables and tasks at the right time.  
  • SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades helps assess your upgrade efforts with analysis of your custom code, compatibility scope usage, and add-on usage.  
  • SAP guidance and resources for technical upgrade execution are included with software subscription., including tasks like operating system upgrade, database upgrade, and the technical installation of SAP S/4HANA upgrades. Please remember that as an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customer, you're responsible for planning upgrades, requesting services, and handling post-upgrade tasks.
  • The Enterprise Support Academy and dedicated SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Value Maps* help upskill your team.  
  • SAP Services and Support including SAP Enterprise Support available with all cloud offerings, and several premium plans, can bring you a dedicated expert assistant for the short or long term.
  • SAP Partner Finder can help you identify a partner from our vast ecosystem for implementation services.  

 

In conclusion: By embracing regular upgrades and a clean core approach, you unlock a multitude of benefits, including accelerated innovation adoption and increased security and compliance. Building your upgrade routine and leveraging valuable tools, services and support from SAP and its ecosystem will result in a smoother upgrade journey.

So don’t let your system reach a point where a costly and time-consuming overhaul becomes essential to avoid slowdown. Maximize the value of your SAP investment - embrace agility and upgrade to stay ahead of the competition.

Read the whitepaper, "Embrace agility and upgrade for success."

Join our Upgrade dedicated SAP Community

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team

entry.title: Be Part of Tomorrow: Join SAP's Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1, 2025!
entry.content:

In today’s fast-paced business world, staying ahead is more important than ever. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative presents a unique opportunity to gain early insights into SAP’s product developments. By participating, you’ll work directly with our developers and product managers, helping shape the future of our products.

Register until March 28, 2025! Don’t miss out on the opportunity to have your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP solutions.

To help you explore the available projects, we’ve compiled a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

We believe that collaboration is key to sparking innovation. By working alongside our customers and partners, we can jointly shape the future of SAP’s products and transform business operations.

Let’s take this journey together. Together, we have the power to drive progress and transform industries.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

entry.title: Import Financial Plan Data - Understanding the Plan Data Scope
entry.content:
The Import Financial Plan Data app is available in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private edition. It allows key users to import plan data line items from CSV files.

Understanding Plan Data Scope

This blog post will explain how the plan data scope works and how it helps you avoid accidently overwriting data.
Please keep in mind that the Import Financial Plan Data app is only an interface for uploading data into the system. The data has to be maintained and reviewed manually before uploading it, as  only technical checks are performed (for example, checking whether master data exists in the target system) and no business logic is integrated.

Let us explore how to use the Import Financial Plan Data app in a simple scenario for cost center planning.

Example Scenarios

Example 1: Initial Import
In the example provided, you intend to import four plan data records. Since this is the first time the application is being used, no records are present in the database. This means four new records will be incorporated from the CSV file, as shown in the following screens:

After importing the plan data, you then see a success message that allows you to run the Display Financial Plan Data report directly. This report displays the plan data on the corresponding accounts, indicating that the outcome is as expected.

Example 2: Correcting Data
Suppose you notice that one of the amounts in your file was incorrect. To correct this, simply change the amount from 100 to 150 USD, and import the file again. The import process informs you that the existing plan data will be adapted within the plan data scope and that four records will be incorporated from the CSV file.

After the successful import, the Display Financial Plan Data report shows the corrected amount of 150 USD.

Example 3: Adding Missing Accounts
In another example, you may have forgotten to upload the plan data for a specific account. In this case, you merely need to reimport the file, including the new account.

The system notifies you that the existing plan data will be adapted within the plan data scope and that five records will be incorporated from the CSV file.
Consequently, the Display Financial Plan Data report now displays the plan data for the missing account as well.

 

Example 4: Handling Complex Scenarios
Let us now examine how the system handles more complex scenarios or repetitive imports.
To reverse specific records before importing a new CSV file, the system creates a logical query based on the dimensions included in the file. Dimensions marked with an X in row #3 are concatenated with AND, while the different members in this column are concatenated with OR. For dimensions without an X, the members are not specified, so records for all members of that dimension are reversed.

Note:
This may reverse data values for which there is no corresponding row in the source file because the selection defined by the plan data scope reverses all values for which the following is true, for example:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025"
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"
AND (Cost Center = "0017101101" OR Cost Center = "17101201")

In example 3, you mistakenly planned on cost center 1710201, which should not have been planned on. The data you imported in this file is incorrect, and now you wish to send only three records instead of five.
To correct this, you reimport the file with these three records, but you leave the X for the cost center dimension in the file.

This action results in the reversal of the records meeting the following criteria:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025"
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"
AND Cost Center = "0017101101"

The erroneous cost center data remains unchanged, so the incorrect data for cost center 1710201 is still present in the system after this import.

To correct the erroneous data, you need to remove the X from the cost center column.

This action results in the reversal of the records meeting the following criteria:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025"
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"

This sets the records for cost centers not specified in the CSV file to zero.

 

Example 5: Updating and Adding Records
In this last example, you want to correct one existing record for 2025 from 150 USD to 200 USD and add new records for 2026.

Let’s assume that the data in your system looks as follows:

By importing the CSV file as shown above, the system reverses the plan records for both cost centers and both years. Note, however, that even records that were not part of the uploaded CSV file are also reversed because of the following criteria:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND (General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025" OR General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2026")
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"
AND (Cost Center = "0017101101" OR Cost Center ="0017101201")

To resolve this, you need to split your data into two upload files.

One CSV file contains the record that needs to be corrected and where the X is set for all dimensions, including the G/L account.

This changes the amount from 150 USD to 200 USD, while all other records remain unchanged.

The second file contains the new records for 2026, where you don’t need to set the X for the G/L account dimension.

After both CSV files have been uploaded, the data in your system looks as follows:

Conclusion:
To prevent data from being overwritten unintentionally, uploaded data must be disjointed. A clear understanding of the plan data scope ensures that records can be added, updated, and reversed safely.

entry.title: Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 03
entry.content:
To complete the blog series about innovations in SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) coming in SAP S/4HANA 2023 (Initial Stack I Feature Pack Stack 01 I Feature Pack Stack 02), I'd now like to share delta information about the key MDG features coming with Feature Pack Stack 03.

Enabling field extensibility in customer and supplier views (cloud-ready mode)

To increase the effectiveness in master data governance of business-partner data in cloud-ready mode, we have enabled the following field extensibility capabilities: 

  • Apply field extensibility (custom fields) not only in the business-partner core view (BUT000), but also in customer and supplier views.
  • Extend fields in the following views:
    • Customer core view (KNA1)
    • Customer company code view (KNB1)
    • Customer sales area core view (KNVV)
    • Supplier core view (LFA1)
    • Supplier company code view (LFB1)
    • Supplier purchasing org view (LFM1)

Field extensibility in supplier view (cloud-ready mode)

Assignment of data controllers to a business partner in the classic mode of central governance

It is now possible to manually assign one or more data controllers to a business partner and set the "Invalid Assignment Status" for existing data controllers. Additionally, the system can immediately derive data controllers when the corresponding organizational units are maintained.

Setting the "Invalid Assignment Status" for existing data controllers

Enhanced validation of changes in financial data 

To avoid errors in the governance of financial data, checks for the most business-critical attributes can be made with remote function calls to target systems to make sure that proposed changes in MDG are validated before change request activation.

Defining target systems for remote validation and display of validation results in MDG processing

Gen AI-based assisted change for custom objects and summary of mass changes*

To further increase the effectiveness through AI in central governance​, we have enabled AI-assisted changes for reuse entities in custom objects and the AI-based summary of change requests with multiple objects (mass changes).

AI-assisted change and AI-based summary of changes

(* Please note that these Gen AI-based features are only available with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition)

See also the presentation outlining the MDG delta content coming with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03.

To get the full picture of planned innovations in SAP Master Data Governance across the entire MDG solution portfolio, see the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management Private Edition 2023 FPS03 – What’s New
entry.content:
We have recently released the latest updates for SAP EWM with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 3 update. The enhancements of this release further improve our Advanced Shipping & Receiving integration and functionalities, complementing the features we delivered in the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 2 release. Furthermore, with this release we introduced a new Machine Learning based feature, called “Predictive Labor Demand Planning”, empowering shift managers at the warehouse to more accurately and efficiently plan out the tasks for the day. And to start today’s deep dive, we will begin with it.

PREDICITVE LABOR DEMAND PLANNING

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release, we deliver the new Machine-Learning based predictive labor demand planning (LDP) that can be used to predict planned workload data based on the picking and packing outbound processes. This enables warehouse shift managers and supervisors to plan resources and workloads more efficiently. Very summarily, LDP uses historical workload records to calculate a planned duration for warehouse workload records, and then use those records to plan outbound delivery orders.

 Historical workload records contain information about executed warehouse tasks (WTs) in the warehouse. They are not stored in an archive as all data is saved in the database, and outdated records are deleted. These records are only written for picking and packing tasks when a warehouse order is confirmed. These records contain application information that is mainly based on warehouse task data, with additional data from the assigned outbound delivery order.

The planned duration is a key component of a planned workload record. In labor management, you can use engineered labor standards to calculate the planned duration, but in labor demand planning, this is not necessary. Instead, labor demand planning uses historical workload records to calculate the planned duration for planned workload records.

Please note that if you have already set up engineered labor standards, they will still be used to calculate the planned duration. In this case, the system will not calculate based on historical workload records. You can also use engineered labor standards for one area of your warehouse and use calculations based on historical workload records for another area.

Outbound delivery order (ODO) processing includes picking and packing steps before the ODO item can be staged and loaded. You can use labor demand planning to get better visibility into the planned workload that is expected from outbound delivery orders. This allows you to predict peaks in the workload and balance the workload of the warehouse accordingly. The way LDP calculates ODO planned workloads is the following:

  1. The system searches for historical data that belongs to similar ODO items. The resulting data forms the basis of the calculations for the required data of the current ODO item.
  2. The system identifies the picking location, preferably by using rough bin determination.
  3. The system determines the external process step that is most used by similar ODO items.
  4. If similar ODO items require packing steps, the system calculates the packing steps based on the packing-picking ratio of the similar ODO items. The packing-picking ratio indicates how often the pick step of an outbound delivery order with similar attributes was followed by a pack step and is calculated from the historical workload data. Single pieces may need a pack step while whole pallet picks don’t need to be packed in addition.

 With LDP, it is possible to view planned workload durations directly in the warehouse monitor, allowing for better visibility and control over task planning. This functionality improves operational efficiency by streamlining the planning process and ensuring more accurate and timely execution of warehouse tasks as well as reduce inefficiencies by predicting the time required for tasks such as picking, packing, and outbound deliveries.

This is only a first step for Predictive Labor Demand Planning, and we plan to build on top of this feature with future releases. But we will reveal those steps at a future date.

ADVANCED SHIPPING & RECEIVING UPDATES

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release we continue to further extend the range of functionalities available with Advanced Shipping & Receiving as well as expand on existing ones. In the interest of time and convenience, I want to briefly touch upon every ASR update in one or two paragraphs, and we begin with integration to Global Trade Service:

  • Integration of Decentralized EWM and TM with GTS – This is a new integration of decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) and Transportation Management (TM) with SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) which supports the execution of the outbound, or export, process. It enables users to conduct compliance checks for freight units and freight orders and complete export declaration for consignments and freight orders.

  • Search for Advanced Shipping and Receiving Deliveries in Waves – In this release, we further build upon a feature we delivered in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, by enabling users to search for waves, wave items, and wave-relevant warehouse requests that are part of Advanced Shipping and Receiving deliveries in the Maintain Waves app (transaction /SCWM/WAVE) and the warehouse monitor (transaction /SCWM/MON).
  • Custom Checks for Loading Materials onto a Freight Order – We also provide BAdIs that add customer-specific checks for handling units or warehouse requests before loading execution in Advanced Shipping and Receiving. The checks take place during outbound delivery, and allow for the customer’s own logic for loading checks in:
    • The radio frequency (RF) screen before loading, where the system can show special warning or error message to the RF user
    • The App "Load or Unload Freight Orders", where the same or a similar BAdI is called when the loading execution function is used
    • The enterprise warehouse management (EWM) desktop transaction /SCWM/LOAD
  • TM Updates for Unpacked Products with Actual EWM Weight and Volume – With this update, when information regarding weight and volume for unpacked products in an outbound delivery order item is updated in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the relevant documents in Transportation Management (TM) are updated with the adjusted EWM weight and volume information. These updates are made after the “Ready for Shipping” status is set in EWM. The updated weight and volume are sent on product item level and on handling unit header level.

CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENTS

To round of today’s overview, I would like to also go over some of the final topics that came to be as a result of the most recent Customer Connect program:

  • Add Short Text to Goods Issue and Cancellation - With this feature, it is now possible to provide a short text for goods issues and cancellations in several apps. A text of up to 50 characters can now be manually entered, for example, to add an explanation or other information to the goods movement in the following apps: “Manage Physical Stock – Products” (F5340); “Manage Warehouse Documents” (F5341). This text can be seen in the relevant material documents in other components of SAP S/4HANA using the “Display Material Document List” app. If synchronous goods movements are used from Inventory Management to Extended Warehouse Management (only available in EWM embedded in SAP S/4HANA), text can be entered in the “Post Goods Movement” app (transaction MIGO). The text is visible in the corresponding goods movement document in EWM, and can be seen in the monitor node at Documents -> Warehouse Task. This functionality has also been added to the consume and scrap actions in the “Warehouse Physical Stock by Product” API.

  • Scanning Alternative HU IDs During RF Processing - With this feature, it is now possible to scan alternative handling unit (HU) IDs on vendor labels which the system converts them to internal Extended Warehouse Management Handling Unit IDs. Converting the alternative HU IDs in RF transactions removes the need to re-label the HUs or create custom RF screens. Note that if multiple HU IDs are assigned to one alternative HU ID, the relevant internal HU ID can be selected.

FIND OUT MORE!

To see more details on the above mentioned topics as well as see what other enhancements we delivered with this release, I highly encourage you to explore our latest “SAP EWM in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 – What’s New” slide deck.

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

For a complete list of Q&A from the EWM community, please access this link:
https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: SAP Field Service Management 2502: Advanced AI, Planning Simulations, and PM Order Integration
entry.content:
SAP Field Service Management 2502 builds on our strategic investment in AI for field service organizations, introducing advanced planning simulations, expanded multilingual AI support, and the ability to query jobs based on user-defined fields (UDFs) and custom label translations.

This release also delivers a native integration with SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) orders using SAP Integration Suite for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, further strengthening customers' integration landscapes.

Additionally, 2502 brings several usability and workflow enhancements, simplifying daily operations while boosting efficiency for planners, dispatchers, and field technicians.

In this blog, we’ll explore the most notable SAP FSM 2502 features, along with demo videos showcasing key highlights.

👉 For the complete list of all features in the 2502 release, click here.

Key Highlights in SAP Field Service Management 2502:

  • Planning Simulations – Review proposed scheduling changes and compare different scheduling policies.
  • Generative AI – Multilingual support (all languages except Tamil) and natural language filtering using UDFs and custom label translations.
  • AI-Based Scheduling – Gain deeper insights into auto-scheduling decisions.
  • Integration – Standard integration with SAP PM orders for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, plus service contract integration.
  • Smartforms Enhancements – Add Smartforms to Service Calls, filter by Smartforms, and edit forms from the Partner Portal.
  • Service Map Enhancements – Support for up to 250 activities/day* and localized units (kilometers/miles).
  • Expanded Language Support – Now supporting Catalan, Croatian, Greek, Kazakh, Serbian (Latin), Slovenian, and Ukrainian.

Note: The Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering features require a customer to be licensed for SAP AI Units. For more information about licensing, please contact your SAP Account Executive.

*Requires HERE Maps routing service.


Artificial Intelligence


Planning Simulations

SAP Field Service Management 2502 introduces Planning Simulations, empowering schedulers to review proposed scheduling changes and compare different scheduling policies before finalizing assignments. This enhancement provides greater control and flexibility, ensuring optimized workforce utilization. Learn more about this feature here.

Planning Simulations

Natural Language Filtering – Additional Language, UDF, & Translation Support

A major enhancement in this release is Natural Language Filtering, which now supports custom label translations and UDF-based searches. This allows organizations to search using company-specific terminology and in their preferred language, enabling a more localized and intuitive AI experience. Find out more details here.

Translation and UDF support for Natural Language Filtering

Generative AI capabilities are now available in all supported languages (except Tamil), making AI-driven insights more accessible to global users. See the full details here.

Expanded multi-lingual support for Generative AI features

AI Impact

According to SAP customer benchmarking, field service organizations leveraging these AI-powered features achieve:

⬆️ 12.5% increase in dispatcher productivity
⬆️5% improvement in first-time fix rates
⬇️ 15% reduction in time spent reviewing and planning service requests

Deeper Insights into Auto-Scheduling Decisions

Reports for AI-based scheduling now offer greater transparency and control, giving planners visibility into technician assignments, travel times, and re-optimization iterations. Detailed scheduling reports provide insights into how decisions are made, improving transparency and increasing autonomy to fine-tune scheduling policies with confidence. Explore this enhancement further here.

Deeper Insights into Auto-Scheduling Decisions


Integration


Integration with Plant Maintenance Orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition

New out-of-the-box integration using SAP Integration Suite connects maintenance planning with scheduling and dispatch, ensuring real-time synchronization between SAP S/4HANA PM orders and SAP FSM. This simplifies integration by reducing custom development efforts and ensuring scalability and consistency across IT landscapes. Read the full details here.

Plant Maintenance order integration with SAP FSM

 Integration flow

Integration of Service Contracts

Service contract synchronization between SAP S/4HANA and SAP FSM ensures real-time access to contract data, SLAs, and response times. This reduces integration complexity, improves performance, and enhances service execution by ensuring field service teams have the right contract details at the right time. Check out the documentation here.

User Experience & Usability Enhancements


Expanded Language Support

SAP FSM now supports Catalan, Croatian, Greek, Kazakh, Serbian (Latin script), Slovenian, and Ukrainian, enabling more users to work in their preferred language. Click here to see all supported languages.

Expanded Language Support - Catalan, Croatian, Greek, Kazakh, Serbian (Latin script), Slovenian, and Ukrainian

 

Master Data Management

Equipment: Improved UX and Measurement Points

With expanded on-screen insights, Equipment Master Data now provides richer details on service history, measurement points, and overdue services, giving users a complete historical view to support well-informed planning. See more here. 

Equipment Master Data Updates

Smartforms

Add Smartforms to Service Calls & Filter by Smartforms

Previously, adding Smartforms to Service Calls or searching for them was not possible, making it difficult to locate specific forms in large lists. Now, Planners and Schedulers can add Smartforms to Service Calls, search for them, and view their status, reducing time spent managing large volumes of forms. For additional insights, click here.

Add Smartforms to Service Calls & Filter by Smartforms

Manage Smartforms in the Partner Portal

Partner Managers can now view, edit, and save Smartforms directly within the Partner Portal, providing greater flexibility when managing technician activities. This enhancement allows Smartforms to be completed on behalf of technicians, improving workflow efficiency and ensuring accurate data validation before closing activities.

Manage Smartforms in the Partner Portal

Service Map

Enhanced Routing with HERE Maps

Dispatchers can now visualize routes for up to 250 activities per day using the HERE Maps routing service, overcoming previous Google Maps limitations. This enhancement improves route planning and visibility, especially when managing high-volume job assignments. More info here.

Enhanced Routing with HERE Maps

Service Map Localization for Distance Units

The Service Map now automatically adjusts distance units based on the user's standard regional preferences, displaying miles or kilometers depending on the region. This enhancement improves usability and localization, ensuring dispatchers and technicians see distances in their preferred unit of measurement. More details here.

Service Map Localization for Distance Units

Final Thoughts

SAP Field Service Management 2502 enhances the entire field service value chain with AI-driven planning simulations, standard SAP S/4HANA PM integration, and usability improvements that simplify daily operations.

We invite you to explore these new capabilities, share your feedback, and help shape the future of SAP FSM by submitting your influence requests here. Stay tuned—even more innovations are on the way in 2505!
